item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells Meet the Press the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond an Instagram post showing seashells arranged to say “86 47,” terminology the Justice Department says threatens the president’s life.

media:content.media:title: Acting AG Todd Blanche says Comey indictment looks beyond ‘a single Instagram post’
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells Meet the Press the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond an Instagram post showing seashells arranged to say “86 47,” terminology the Justice Department says threatens the president’s life.

item.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche says Comey indictment looks beyond ‘a single Instagram post’

item.description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

media:content.media:title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines announced in a statement that it would wind down operations immediately, canceling all flights, over rising jet fuel costs and struggling with profitability. NBC News’ Jay Blackman reports on how the decision will impact thousands of employees and travelers.

item.title: Spirit Airlines announces all flights are canceled as it shuts down operations

item.dc:creator: Jay Ganglani
item.description: A photo exhibition offering a rare look into industrial fishing in Southeast Asia — and its impact on fragile marine ecosystems — was on display at a New York gallery last month.

media:content.media:text: Tanjung Luar is one of the largest shark markets in Indonesia and Southeast Asia, from where shark fins are exported to other Asian markets, primarily Hong Kong and China, was bones are used in cosmetic products also sold to China. Shark meat and skins are consumed locally as an important source of protein. In recent years, facing heavy criticism because of the unregulated shark fishing industry, the Indonesian government has sought to bring in stricter controls over commercial hunting of sharks in an attempt to balance the needs of fishermen as well as the need to protect dwindling shark populations.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Many filipino commercial and small-scale fishers are being chased or hassled by Chinese Coast Guard, Navy, and militia ships while out at sea. Previously, attacks or harassment in this area did not exist, but was well-documented in other areas. Since August 2024, incidences of harassment have started and increased since. Many Filipino fishermen believe that the Chinese are now moving to build outposts in the nearby Sabina Shoal to dominate both trade routes and the fishing industry.
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

media:content.media:text: Overfishing in SE Asia
media:content.media:description: Various species of sharks, some of which are endangered, being hauled on shore at dawn by commercial fishermen at the Tanjung Luar port in East Lombok, Indonesia, in June 2025.

item.title: Photo essay: Southeast Asia's overfishing crisis — and the effort to uncover its roots

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Kimi Antonelli won the Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix on Sunday, securing his third victory in a row and increasing his lead in the world championship.

media:content.media:text: Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Italy driving the Mercedes AMG Petronas F1 Team W17 on track during the F1 Grand Prix of Miami at Miami International Autodrome on May 3, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Mercedes driver Kimi Antonelli holds the trophy as he celebrates on the podium after the 2026 Miami Formula 1 Grand Prix on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Wide view of cars on a racetrack
media:content.media:description: Mercedes driver Kimi Antonelli holds the trophy as he celebrates on the podium after the 2026 Miami Formula 1 Grand Prix on Sunday.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix, extending his lead in the standings

media:content.media:description: Abortion pill maker asks to restore mail access

item.title: Abortion pill maker asks to restore mail access

item.description: Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California says that while multiple presidents have not gone to Congress for war authorization in the past, President Trump’s actions are a “clear violation” of the War Powers Act of 1973.

media:content.media:title: Senator says Trump's actions violates war powers law
media:content.media:description: Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California says that while multiple presidents have not gone to Congress for war authorization in the past, President Trump’s actions are a “clear violation” of the War Powers Act of 1973.

item.title: Senator says Trump's actions violates war powers law

item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche reacts to President Trump saying he doesn't use the word "war" when referring to the U.S. conflict with Iran.

media:content.media:title: DOJ chief says U.S. not actively at war with Iran
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche reacts to President Trump saying he doesn't use the word "war" when referring to the U.S. conflict with Iran.

item.title: DOJ chief says U.S. not actively at war with Iran

item.title: Driver crashes through Portland club

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Joe Kottke
item.description: After weeks of viral “speed running” videos filmed at the Church of Scientology’s Hollywood building have garnered millions of views, a similar stunt appears to have been attempted in New York

media:content.media:text: A brick building with tall windows and an awning with lettering: CHURCH OF SCIENTOLOGY OF NEW YORK.
media:content.media:description: The Church of Scientology of New York in 2017. On Saturday, a group of individuals gained entry to the midtown Manhattan church after breaking through a locked door.

item.title: Viral 'speed running' challenge hits Church of Scientology New York

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: A hidden force is quietly pushing up costs for everything from your summer vacation to your weekly grocery bills: a weaker U.S. dollar

media:content.media:text: A person holds U.S. dollar bills in his hand on Sept. 6, 2023.
media:content.media:description: A strong dollar makes imports cheaper and can help keep inflation in check.

item.title: How a weaker dollar is quietly making life more expensive

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
item.description: Kimi Antonelli won the Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix on Sunday, securing his third victory in a row and increasing his lead in the world championship.

media:content.media:text: Andrea Kimi Antonelli of Italy driving the Mercedes AMG Petronas F1 Team W17 on track during the F1 Grand Prix of Miami at Miami International Autodrome on May 3, 2026.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix, extending his lead in the standings

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: Not too long after James Gunn and Peter Safran stepped up to lead DC Studios into the future, they were riffing about Supergirl.

media:content.media:text: Warner Bros. "The Big Picture" Cinema Con 2026
media:content.media:description: Milly Alcock attends Warner Bros. "The Big Picture" CinemaCon 2026 at Caesars Palace, in Las Vegas, on April 14.

item.title: Milly Alcock's 'punk rock' Supergirl takes flight as DC bets big on the Woman of Tomorrow

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: A United Airlines passenger allegedly attacked a crew member and attempted to access the cockpit before being removed by police at Newark Liberty International Airport on Saturday evening

media:content.media:text: United Airlines
media:content.media:description: A United Airlines aircraft.

item.title: United Airlines passenger allegedly assaulted flight attendant, tried to get into cockpit

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: One person was killed and three others were injured in a shooting at post-prom party in Indianapolis on Sunday morning, police said

media:content.media:text: Police cars parked on a residential street behind police tape
media:content.media:description: A person is dead and three people are injured after a shooting at a post-prom party at a rental home in Indianapolis early Sunday morning.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 injured in shooting at post-prom gathering in Indianapolis

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
Austin Mullen
Christine Rapp
item.description: Two people have drowned in Florida amid days of heavy rain, damaging winds and “extremely rough” ocean conditions, according to local officials

media:content.media:text: Photo shows a beachgoers sun canopy flapping in the strong winds at Tables Beach
media:content.media:description: Strong winds affect beachgoers at Tables Beach in Cocoa Park, Fla., on April 27.

media:content.media:text: A general view of the pitch during an inclement weather delay
media:content.media:description: Strong winds affect beachgoers at Tables Beach in Cocoa Park, Fla., on April 27.

item.title: 2 killed in Florida amid strong winds and 'extremely rough' ocean conditions
